feature_sets.name: User authentication
feature_sets.goal: Sign up, sign in, sign out, recover. Solid auth across email/password and major OAuth providers.
feature_sets.rationale: Every app needs a login the user can trust. This set covers the full account lifecycle from registration through password recovery and third-party sign-in.

feature_sets.name: Search and discovery
feature_sets.goal: Help users find what they came for. Full-text search, filters, pagination, recommendations.
feature_sets.rationale: A populated app is useful only if users can navigate it. This set covers the discovery surface: search, filtering, pagination, and the recommendation engine that surfaces what a user didn't know to look for.

tickets.title: Sign in with email + password
tickets.goal: Authenticated users can sign in with their email and password.
tickets.why: Foundation for every other authenticated feature.
done_when: POST /auth/login validates credentials against bcrypt-hashed passwords
A signed JWT is returned on success
Wrong password returns 401 with a generic error
Rate limit: 10 attempts per 15 minutes per IP
tickets.notes: JWT over sessions for stateless API compat. Refresh-token flow tracked in CM-003.

tickets.title: User registration
tickets.goal: A new user can create an account with email and password.
tickets.why: Without registration there's no one to sign in.
done_when: POST /auth/register accepts email + password + name
Password meets policy (min 12 chars, mixed case, digit)
Email uniqueness enforced at the DB layer
Account starts in pending state until verified
tickets.notes: Email verification handled separately in CM-007.

tickets.title: Session management
tickets.goal: Issued JWTs are validated, refreshed, and revoked correctly.
tickets.why: A robust auth surface needs more than 'is this token signed by us?' — refresh, revocation on logout, short-lived access tokens.
done_when: Access tokens TTL = 15 min, refresh tokens TTL = 30 days
POST /auth/refresh exchanges a valid refresh token for a new access token
POST /auth/logout revokes the refresh token
Middleware rejects expired or revoked tokens with 401
tickets.notes: Used Redis for the revocation list — TTL self-prunes.

tickets.title: Profile page
tickets.goal: Signed-in users can view and edit their profile.
tickets.why: First place a user lands after sign-in. Empty state would suggest the app is broken.
done_when: GET /profile returns the user's record
PATCH /profile accepts partial updates
Avatar upload accepts images <= 2MB
Email change triggers re-verification

tickets.title: OAuth Google integration
tickets.goal: Users can sign in with their Google account.
tickets.why: Reduces signup friction by ~40%. Critical for mobile.
done_when: 'Continue with Google' button on the login page
OAuth 2.0 redirect flow implemented end-to-end
New Google sign-ins create a user record automatically
Existing accounts can link a Google identity from their profile
tickets.notes: PKCE flow, no client secret in the SPA.

tickets.title: Password reset flow
tickets.goal: Users who forget their password can reset it via email.
tickets.why: Top-3 support ticket category in any signed-in app.
done_when: POST /auth/forgot accepts an email; always returns 200 (no leak)
Reset email contains a single-use token (24-hour expiry)
POST /auth/reset accepts token + new password
All active sessions revoked on successful reset

tickets.title: Email verification
tickets.goal: New registrations and email changes require a verification click.
tickets.why: Stops typo'd or fraudulent registrations from blocking the email forever.
done_when: Registration sends a verification email with a 24-hour token
Unverified accounts cannot post user-generated content
Email change re-issues verification
Resend-verification endpoint with rate limit

tickets.title: Sign in with Apple
tickets.goal: Users can sign in with their Apple ID.
tickets.why: Apple's Sign In with Apple is mandatory once we ship iOS with Google OAuth — App Store will reject the build otherwise.
done_when: 'Sign in with Apple' button on login + registration
Apple's 'hide my email' relay supported
Web (SIWA JS) and native iOS flows both work

tickets.title: Two-factor authentication
tickets.goal: Users can opt in to TOTP-based 2FA on their account.
tickets.why: Required by enterprise prospects. Also a strong trust signal for security-conscious consumers.
done_when: Profile setting to enable/disable 2FA
TOTP setup flow with QR code + recovery codes
Login enforces second factor when enabled
Recovery code can be used in place of TOTP (one-time)

tickets.title: Basic search
tickets.goal: Users can run a full-text search across the catalog.
tickets.why: The single most-used surface in any content-heavy app.
done_when: GET /search?q=<query> returns top 25 hits
Postgres FTS index in place; query returns < 200ms p95
Empty query returns empty result set, not 400
Special characters escaped before being passed to ts_query

tickets.title: Search filters
tickets.goal: Users can narrow search results by category, date range, and price range.
tickets.why: A 25-result page without filters is just a wall.
done_when: Query params: category, min_date, max_date, min_price, max_price
Filters compose (AND across types)
Filter chips render below the search bar
Result count updates live as filters change

tickets.title: Search results pagination
tickets.goal: Users can page through more than the first 25 search results.
tickets.why: Limiting to 25 silently drops the long tail.
done_when: cursor query param accepts an opaque token
Each page returns next_cursor (null when done)
'Load more' button advances the cursor without leaving the page

tickets.title: Search ranking algorithm
tickets.goal: Replace lexical-only ranking with a hybrid score (BM25 + recency + popularity).
tickets.why: Pure lexical match buries fresh, popular content under stale-but-keyword-perfect matches.
done_when: Score formula: 0.6 * BM25 + 0.25 * recency_decay + 0.15 * normalized_clicks
Recency decay half-life: 30 days
A/B harness on a 50/50 split
p95 query latency stays under 250ms

tickets.title: Recommendation engine v2
tickets.goal: 'You might also like' driven by collaborative filtering, replacing the rule-based recommender.
tickets.why: Current recommender clicks at <2%. Industry benchmark for CF is 6-8%.
done_when: ALS model trained nightly on 30-day interaction window
/recommendations/<user_id> returns top 10 unseen items
Cold-start fallback: trending in user's stated categories
CTR measured against v1 via A/B at 5%
tickets.notes: ALS over neural CF for v2 — trains in 8 min, deploys via batch export. Neural CF is overkill until catalog grows 10x.

tickets.title: Search synonyms support
tickets.goal: 'sneakers' and 'trainers' return the same results. So do 'couch' and 'sofa'.
tickets.why: Users from different markets use different words for the same thing.
done_when: Synonym dictionary maintained as a config file
Postgres FTS configured with the synonym dictionary
Adding a synonym pair requires only a redeploy

tickets.title: Trending searches widget
tickets.goal: Empty search bar shows the top 5 most-run searches in the last 24 hours.
tickets.why: Idle users with nothing to search for hit a dead end at the empty search page.
done_when: Materialized view rebuilt every 15 minutes
'Trending' chips render below the search bar when input is empty
Clicking a chip pre-fills the search and runs it
Privacy: only show trending if at least 50 unique users issued the query
